summary.intent: Fix a Jenkins layered-products build failure where oadp-operator was triggered despite referencing oadp-velero (a disabled image), causing the build to crash during rebase.
summary.outcome: Implemented disabled dependency detection in scan-sources, made layered-products scan pipeline filter such images, improved error messages, added tests, created PR #2710, and reverted unnecessary ocp4-scan changes per user feedback. User now requesting to scope fix to non-openshift groups only.

repo: art-tools repo uses origin (fork) and prod (upstream) remote pattern
Image metadata YAMLs have 'mode: disabled' and 'dependents' fields that establish dependency links
name_in_bundle_map is populated from ALL image YAMLs regardless of enabled/disabled status
Scan pipelines generate reports that trigger build pipelines; reports can have flags like okd_only, has_disabled_dependency

code.finding: First load_data(path='images') loads ALL YAMLs for name maps; second call with keys/exclude filters to enabled subset
Per-image error handling uses return_exceptions=True to collect failures without crashing entire batch
File uses local imports for DoozerFatalError rather than top-level imports

workflow: Jenkins MCP server requires useRegex: true for regex pattern matching in logs
Follow existing pipeline patterns (ocp4_scan_konflux.py) when implementing similar features
Git workflow: branch from prod/main, commit, push to fork, PR to upstream
Use git commit --amend + force-push to revise commits before they reach shared branches
pytest for pyartcd tests must run from pyartcd directory
MagicMock(spec=Runtime) doesn't recognize dynamically-assigned attributes like gitdata; need explicit setup

friction: Jenkins MCP search failed initially with useRegex: false, required retry
Chicken-and-egg understanding: metadata deps vs CSV deps vs source code image-references took multiple investigation rounds
Unnecessary scope creep: ocp4_scan_konflux.py change questioned by user and reverted
open_items: User's final request: apply disabled dependency filtering only for non-openshift groups (implementation pending)
Cannot verify with actual dry-run against Red Hat infrastructure; relying on unit tests only
Disabled dependency check reads ALL image YAMLs - potential performance implications for large groups not measured
